evidence: V: 'So the demo is like human data. Like it's a human data oriented demo.' - Crystallized positioning: Careerspan sells human data, not software.

Logan: 'Boom, mic drop for the hiring people. This is opted in candidate data... as things heat up with publicly scraped data, this is a really valuable strategy for you guys.' - Eightfold lawsuit as hook.
Logan articulated the regulatory moment around Eightfold lawsuit as a sales hook for first-party data positioning

Logan: 'Can we make like a 30 second look at the information that we capture via Careerspan? Because if that's in an email... they don't have to wrap their heads around what it is we're describing. It's just like oh shit, I want that information.'
Logan: 'It's like kind of a baller move to put the lead list of workforce boards in. That's kind of like just giving value up front.'
Zain: 'Oh, wow. Okay. That is a small world. I had no idea. Yeah, that's a random connection because we got connected through Zo.' - Serendipitous TikTok connection to V's co-founder.
Zain: 'how do we make sure or how do we ensure that as we have more automation in workplace, as we see more AI and adoption, how do we ensure that's actually good for us as humans'
V: 'that's highly congruent' - Both theses focus on AI augmenting human capability vs. replacing it.
B32: 'V described himself as becoming a 1% or 0.1% power user... yet identifies as non-technical. Is there a fundamental mismatch between how we define technical vs. power user?'
B32: 'Perhaps frequent relocation doesn't just make you adaptable—it makes you good at noticing adaptation... practiced at recognizing when to stop trying to adapt and start trying to belong.'
B32: 'We optimize for efficiency, but trust is created by inefficiency. The small world moments are valuable precisely because they weren't supposed to happen.'
Connection to V's cornerstone: inefficiency creates latency which builds trust - opposite of algorithmic filtering
B32: 'The monitor isn't about doing more—it's about being the kind of person who cares about the details. Maybe all productivity is just identity performance.'

B32: 'maybe authenticity isn't a competitive strategy—it's a defensive one. Authentic content creates trust slowly and transactionally, whereas clickbait extracts attention quickly and parasitically.'
V: 'I have long been of the belief that, like, if you have substantive things to say, you don't need to rely on these like, cheap tactics.'
Zain: 'One of my beliefs too is like, I mean for most part it's like collaboration over competition.'
V: 'I'm actually about to be announced as a co lead for nyc. So my other co lead is. Is also part of group so she would be really cool to intro you too.' - V recommending Zain for FOHE membership.

Zain: 'collaboration over competition. I think there's just so much opportunity, especially for people who are looking at it from this angle... most of the startups I see are focusing a lot on automation piece, but they're not necessarily looking at it with a human centered lens.'
